Built with Alectryon, running Coq+SerAPI v8.16.0+0.16.0. Bubbles () indicate interactive fragments: hover for details, tap to reveal contents. Use Ctrl+↑ Ctrl+↓ to navigate, Ctrl+🖱️ to focus. On Mac, use ⌘ instead of Ctrl.
(** * Verif_append1: List segments *)

(** Here is a little C program, [append.c]

    #include <stddef.h>

    struct list {int head; struct list *tail;};

    struct list *append (struct list *x, struct list *y) {
      struct list *t, *u;
      if (x==NULL)
        return y;
      else {
        t = x;
        u = t->tail;
        while (u!=NULL) {
          t = u;
          u = t->tail;
        }
        t->tail = y;
        return x;
      }
    }

In this chapter, we will learn to verify this in-place linked-list-append
operation. That is, two original lists will be destructed and a new list,
the concatenation of them, will be constructed in this program.
As we did in previous chapters, we import VST and the C program's AST. *)

Require VC.Preface. (* Check for the right version of VST *)

Require Import VST.floyd.proofauto.
Require Import VC.append.

compspecs
 make_compspecs prog. Defined.

varspecs
 mk_varspecs prog. Defined.

(* ================================================================= *)
(** ** Specification of the [append] function. *)

(** Here we just copy what we have defined in [Verif_reverse] *)

Definition t_list := Tstruct _list noattr.

Fixpoint listrep (sigma: list val) (p: val) : mpred :=
 match sigma with
 | h::hs => 
    EX y:val, 
      data_at Tsh t_list (h,y) p  *  listrep hs y
 | nil => 
    !! (p = nullval) && emp
 end.

Arguments listrep sigma p : simpl never.

(** Then we can easily describe the functionality of this [append]. *)

Definition append_spec :=
 DECLARE _append
  WITH x: val, y: val, s1: list val, s2: list val
  PRE [ tptr t_list , tptr t_list]
     PROP()
     PARAMS (x; y)
     SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y)
  POST [ tptr t_list ]
    EX r: val,
     PROP()
     RETURN(r)
     SEP (listrep (s1++s2) r).

Definition Gprog : funspecs := [ append_spec ].

(* ================================================================= *)
(** ** List segments. *)

(** When verifying this program, a critical step is to figure out
a correct loop invariant. If we try to simulate this program,
especially the loop in it, we may want a loop invariant which
can be illustrated by the following diagram.
(The following diagram is best demonstrated with a monospaced font. )

        +---+---+            +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   
  x ==> |   |  ===> ... ===> |   | t ==> | b | u ==> |   |  ===> ... 
        +---+---+            +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+

      | <========= s1a =========> |       (b)       | <==== s1c ====> |
      | <============================= s1 ==========================> |

        +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   
  y ==> |   |  ===> |   |   ==> |   |  ===> ... 
        +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+

      | <================ s2 =================> |
*)

(** To describe this loop invariant, we need a separation logic predicate to
describe the partial linked list from address [x] to address [t] with contents
[s1a]. This must a new predicate different from [listrep] because [listrep]
describes linked lists ending with [NULL] which is not the case here. We call
this new predicate [lseg], pronounced "list-segment". *)

Fixpoint lseg (contents: list val) (x z: val) : mpred :=
  match contents with
  | nil => !! (x = z) && emp
  | h::hs => EX y:val, data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x * lseg hs y z
  end.

Arguments lseg contents x z : simpl never.

(** Now, we can prove some useful properties about [lseg]. *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard (singleton_lseg) *)

forall a x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x |-- lseg [a] x y


forall a x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x |-- lseg [a] x y

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** It is critical to observe that a partial linked list defined by
 [lseg s x y] may have a loop. For example, the following diagram does
 satisfy [lseg [a; b] x y].
(The following diagram is best demonstrated with a monospaced font. )

        +---+---+   +---+---+   
  x ==> | a | y ==> | b | y ====+ 
        +---+---+   +- -+---+   |
                      ^         |
                      |         |
                      +=========+

We can prove this formally. *)


forall a b x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y |-- lseg [a; b] x y


forall a b x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y |-- lseg [a; b] x y

  
a, b, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y |-- lseg [a; b] x y

  
a, b, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
|-- EX y0 : val,
    data_at Tsh t_list (a, y0) x
      * (EX y1 : val,
         data_at Tsh t_list (b, y1) y0
           * (!! (y1 = y) && emp))

  
a, b, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
|-- data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
      * (EX y0 : val,
         data_at Tsh t_list (b, y0) y
           * (!! (y0 = y) && emp))

  
a, b, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
|-- data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
      * (data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
           * (!! (y = y) && emp))

  entailer!.
Qed.

(** Is our definition of [lseg] wrong? The answer is no because a loopy [lseg]
cannot connect to a nonempty linked list. For instance, we can prove that

  data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y * listrep [c] y

will lead to a contradication. Here, the first two separating conjuncts build
a loopy [lseg] and the third separating conjunct is a nonempty [listrep]. *)


forall a b c x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * listrep [c] y |-- FF


forall a b c x y : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * listrep [c] y |-- FF

  
a, b, c, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * listrep [c] y |-- FF

  
a, b, c, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * (EX y0 : val,
     data_at Tsh t_list (c, y0) y
       * (!! (y0 = nullval) && emp)) |-- FF

  
a, b, c, x, y, u: val
H: u = nullval
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * (data_at Tsh t_list (c, u) y * emp) |-- FF

  
a, b, c, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * (data_at Tsh t_list (c, nullval) y * emp) |-- FF


data_at_conflict Tsh t_list (c, nullval)
     : forall (v' : reptype t_list) (p : val),
       sepalg.nonidentity Tsh ->
       0 < sizeof t_list ->
       data_at Tsh t_list (c, nullval) p
         * data_at Tsh t_list v' p |-- FF
a, b, c, x, y: val
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, y) y
  * (data_at Tsh t_list (c, nullval) y * emp) |-- FF
 
  
a, b, c, x, y: val
sepalg.nonidentity Tsh
a, b, c, x, y: val
FF * data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x |-- FF
 
  
a, b, c, x, y: val
sepalg.nonidentity Tsh
 auto.
  
a, b, c, x, y: val
FF * data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x |-- FF
 entailer!.
Qed.

(** Important note!  The proof above demonstrates the use of the [sep_apply]
 tactic.  Step through that part of the proof to see what [sep_apply] does. *)

(** Now we can prove the following theorems about partial linked
 lists and complete linked lists. *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard (lseg_lseg) *)

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y z : val),
lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z |-- lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y z : val),
lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z |-- lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard (lseg_list) *)

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y : val),
lseg s1 x y * listrep s2 y |-- listrep (s1 ++ s2) x


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y : val),
lseg s1 x y * listrep s2 y |-- listrep (s1 ++ s2) x

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** Is it possible to define [lseg] in a different way so that loopy 
   situations can be banned?  Yes.  We discuss this near the end of 
   the chapter. *)

(* ================================================================= *)
(** ** Proof of the [append] function *)

(** Before verifying the functional correctness of [append], we still need
to add lemmas to hint databases for separation logic predicates. Readers
may copy proofs from [Verif_reverse] or just skip down to "End of the
material". *)


forall (sigma : list val) (p : val),
listrep sigma p
|-- !! (is_pointer_or_null p /\
        (p = nullval <-> sigma = []))


forall (sigma : list val) (p : val),
listrep sigma p
|-- !! (is_pointer_or_null p /\
        (p = nullval <-> sigma = []))

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
#[export] Hint Resolve listrep_local_facts : saturate_local.


forall (sigma : list val) (p : val),
listrep sigma p |-- valid_pointer p


forall (sigma : list val) (p : val),
listrep sigma p |-- valid_pointer p

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
#[export] Hint Resolve listrep_valid_pointer : valid_pointer.

(** (End of the material repeated from Verif_reverse.v) *)

(** In C programs, we test whether the head pointer of a linked list is null
 to determine whether that list is empty or not. Thus, from a separating
 conjunct [listrep contents x], it is useful to prove [contents = nil] (or
 [contents <> nil]) when knowing that [x = nullval] (or [x <> nullval]). The
 following two lemmas state such correlation. They will be used several
 times in the C function [append]'s correctness proof. *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard (listrep_null) *)

forall (contents : list val) (x : val),
x = nullval ->
listrep contents x = !! (contents = []) && emp


forall (contents : list val) (x : val),
x = nullval ->
listrep contents x = !! (contents = []) && emp

(** Hint: One way to prove [P=Q], where P and Q are [mpred]s,
  is to apply [pred_ext] and then prove [P|--Q] and [Q|--P]. *)
(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard (listrep_nonnull) *)

forall (contents : list val) (x : val),
x <> nullval ->
listrep contents x = EX (h : val) (hs : list val)
                     (y : val),
                     !! (contents = h :: hs) &&
                     data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x
                       * listrep hs y


forall (contents : list val) (x : val),
x <> nullval ->
listrep contents x = EX (h : val) (hs : list val)
                     (y : val),
                     !! (contents = h :: hs) &&
                     data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x
                       * listrep hs y

(** Again, [pred_ext] will be useful here. *)
(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** Now, let's prove this [append] function correct. *)

(** **** Exercise: 3 stars, standard (body_append) *)

semax_body Vprog Gprog f_append append_spec


semax_body Vprog Gprog f_append append_spec


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (if (_x == (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
     return _y;
   } else {
     (_t = _x;
      _u = (_t -> _tail);
      while (_u != (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
        (_t = _u;
         _u = (_t -> _tail);)
      }
      (_t -> _tail) = _y;
      return _x;)
   }) POSTCONDITION


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y)) 
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y)) 
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION

  (** This if-then branch handles the cases in which [x] is null. In other
   words, [s1] should be [nil]. We can easily derive this by [listrep_null].
   The rest of the proof in this branch is left as an exercise. *)
  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (!! (s1 = []) && emp; listrep s2 y))
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION

  (* FILL IN HERE *) admit.

Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  (** This time, we know that [x] is not null; thus [s1] should
    be nonempty. *)
  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (EX (h : val) (hs : list val) 
        (y : val),
        !! (s1 = h :: hs) &&
        data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x
          * listrep hs y; 
   listrep s2 y)) (_t = _x;
                   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y)) 
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _t x; temp _x x; 
   temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y))
  (_u = (_t -> _tail);
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
LOOP_BODY:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
   temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y))
  (while (_u != (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
     LOOP_BODY
   }
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION


  (** After symbolically executing two assignment commands, we arrive
     at the while loop. As mentioned above, we can verify it using
     the following loop invariant. *)
  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
listrep r u; listrep s2 y)
|-- EX (s1a : list val) 
    (b : val) (s1c : list val) 
    (t u : val),
    PROP (s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c)
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
    temp _u u; temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
    listrep s1c u; listrep s2 y)
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP (s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c)
LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
temp _u u0; temp _y y)
SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y)
|-- tc_expr Delta (!_u != (tptr tvoid) (0))%expr
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
HRE: u0 <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u0; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
   listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _u;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
HRE: u0 = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u0; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
   listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y))
  ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
listrep r u; listrep s2 y)
|-- EX (s1a : list val) 
    (b : val) (s1c : list val) 
    (t u : val),
    PROP (s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c)
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
    temp _u u; temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
    listrep s1c u; listrep s2 y)

      
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
listrep r u; listrep s2 y)
|-- PROP (s1 = [] ++ h :: r)
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t x; 
    temp _u u; temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg [] x x; data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x;
    listrep r u; listrep s2 y)

      
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
listrep r u; listrep s2 y)
|-- PROP (h :: r = [] ++ h :: r)
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t x; 
    temp _u u; temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg [] x x; data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x;
    listrep r u; listrep s2 y)
 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
PNu: is_pointer_or_null u
PNx: is_pointer_or_null x
PNy: is_pointer_or_null y
H0: field_compatible t_list [] x
H1: value_fits t_list (h, u)
H2: u = nullval <-> r = []
H3: y = nullval <-> s2 = []
emp |-- lseg [] x x
 unfold lseg; entailer!.
 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP (s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c)
LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
temp _u u0; temp _y y)
SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y)
|-- tc_expr Delta (!_u != (tptr tvoid) (0))%expr

      entailer!.
 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
HRE: u0 <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u0; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
   listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _u;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

    (** We know [u] is not null from the fact that the loop condition
       is true. Thus we can represent [s1c] in the form of [(c :: s1d)]. *)
    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   listrep s1c u; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _u;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   EX (h : val) (hs : list val) 
   (y : val),
   !! (s1c = h :: hs) && data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) u
     * listrep hs y; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _u;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
H0: s1c = c :: s1d
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
   listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _u;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
H0: s1c = c :: s1d
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _t u; temp _x x; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
   listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y)) 
  (_u = (_t -> _tail);) POSTCONDITION

    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
H0: s1c = c :: s1d
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u z; temp _t u; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y)
|-- EX a : list val * val * list val * val * val,
    PROP (s1 = fst (fst (fst (fst a))) ++
               snd (fst (fst (fst a)))
               :: snd (fst (fst a)))
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t (snd (fst a));
    temp _u (snd a); temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg (fst (fst (fst (fst a)))) x
           (snd (fst a));
    data_at Tsh t_list
      (snd (fst (fst (fst a))), snd a) 
      (snd (fst a));
    listrep (snd (fst (fst a))) (snd a); 
    listrep s2 y)


    (** In the end of the loop body, we need to re-establish the
      loop invariant. At this point, the memory layout can be
      illustrated by the following diagram.

      (The following diagram is best demonstrated with a monospaced font. )

                                 new t       new u
                                   |           |
                                   |           |
                 +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   
  x ==> ... ===> |   | t ==> | b | u ==> | c | z ==> |   |  ===> ... 
                 +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+

      | <===== s1a =====> |   (b)         (c)      | <==== s1d ====> |
      | <========== new s1a ========> |  (new b)   | <== new s1c ==> |

        +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   
  y ==> |   |  ===> |   |   ==> |   |  ===> ... 
        +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+
      | <================ s2 =================> |

 Clearly, [s1a ++ b :: nil] should be the new value of [s1a];  [c] should be
 the new value of [b]; [s1d] should be the new value of [s1c]; [u] should be
 the new value of [t]; and [z] should be the new value of [u]. The next 
 command instantiates the existentially quantified variables in our loop
 invariant accordingly. *)

   
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
H0: s1c = c :: s1d
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u z; temp _t u; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y)
|-- PROP (s1 = fst
                 (fst
                    (fst
                       (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)))) ++
               snd
                 (fst
                    (fst
                       (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z))))
               :: snd
                    (fst
                       (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z))))
    LOCAL (temp _x x;
    temp _t (snd (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)));
    temp _u (snd (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z));
    temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg
           (fst
              (fst
                 (fst (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)))))
           x (snd (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)));
    data_at Tsh t_list
      (snd
         (fst (fst (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)))),
       snd (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z))
      (snd (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)));
    listrep
      (snd (fst (fst (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z))))
      (snd (s1a ++ [b], c, s1d, u, z)); 
    listrep s2 y)
 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
H0: s1c = c :: s1d
ENTAIL Delta,
PROP ( )
LOCAL (temp _u z; temp _t u; 
temp _x x; temp _y y)
SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y)
|-- PROP (s1 = (s1a ++ [b]) ++ c :: s1d)
    LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t u; 
    temp _u z; temp _y y)
    SEP (lseg (s1a ++ [b]) x u;
    data_at Tsh t_list (c, z) u; 
    listrep s1d z; listrep s2 y)


  (** As usual, we try [entailer!] to solve this proof goal. This time,
 [entailer!] does not solve it directly. Instead, two simplified proof goals
 are left. Their proofs are left for the reader, using [app_assoc], 
 [singleton_lseg] and [lseg_lseg]. *)
    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b, t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
PNz: is_pointer_or_null z
PNu: is_pointer_or_null u
PNx: is_pointer_or_null x
PNy: is_pointer_or_null y
H2: field_compatible t_list [] t
H3: value_fits t_list (b, u)
H4: field_compatible t_list [] u
H5: value_fits t_list (c, z)
H6: z = nullval <-> s1d = []
H7: y = nullval <-> s2 = []
s1a ++ b :: c :: s1d = (s1a ++ [b]) ++ c :: s1d
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b, t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
PNz: is_pointer_or_null z
PNu: is_pointer_or_null u
PNx: is_pointer_or_null x
PNy: is_pointer_or_null y
H2: field_compatible t_list [] t
H3: value_fits t_list (b, u)
H4: field_compatible t_list [] u
H5: value_fits t_list (c, z)
H6: z = nullval <-> s1d = []
H7: y = nullval <-> s2 = []
lseg s1a x t * data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t
|-- lseg (s1a ++ [b]) x u

    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b, t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
PNz: is_pointer_or_null z
PNu: is_pointer_or_null u
PNx: is_pointer_or_null x
PNy: is_pointer_or_null y
H2: field_compatible t_list [] t
H3: value_fits t_list (b, u)
H4: field_compatible t_list [] u
H5: value_fits t_list (c, z)
H6: z = nullval <-> s1d = []
H7: y = nullval <-> s2 = []
s1a ++ b :: c :: s1d = (s1a ++ [b]) ++ c :: s1d
 admit.
    
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b, t, u: val
HRE: u <> nullval
c: val
s1d: list val
z: val
PNz: is_pointer_or_null z
PNu: is_pointer_or_null u
PNx: is_pointer_or_null x
PNy: is_pointer_or_null y
H2: field_compatible t_list [] t
H3: value_fits t_list (b, u)
H4: field_compatible t_list [] u
H5: value_fits t_list (c, z)
H6: z = nullval <-> s1d = []
H7: y = nullval <-> s2 = []
lseg s1a x t * data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t
|-- lseg (s1a ++ [b]) x u
 admit.
 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u0: val
HRE: u0 = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u0; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u0) t;
   listrep s1c u0; listrep s2 y))
  ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

   (** After exiting the loop, the loop condition must be false, i.e.
    [u] is the null pointer. Thus [s1c = nil] and [s1 = s1a ++ [b]]. *)    
  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   listrep s1c u; listrep s2 y))
  ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   !! (s1c = []) && emp; 
   listrep s2 y)) ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
                   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b: val
s1c: list val
t, u: val
HRE: u = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ b :: s1c
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
H0: s1c = []
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   emp; listrep s2 y))
  ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

  
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
s1a: list val
b, t, u: val
HRE: u = nullval
H1: s1 = s1a ++ [b]
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _t t; 
   temp _u u; temp _y y)
   SEP (lseg s1a x t; data_at Tsh t_list (b, u) t;
   emp; listrep s2 y))
  ((_t -> _tail) = _y;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION


  (** The rest of the proof is standard. Hint, [singleton_lseg],
    [lseg_lseg] and/or [lseg_list] may be useful. *)
  (* FILL IN HERE *) admit.
(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(* ================================================================= *)
(** ** Additional exercises: more proofs about list segments *)

(** For verifying the C function [append], it is enough to have only three
 separation logic proof rules about [lseg]: [singleton_lseg], [lseg_lseg]
 and [lseg_list]. The following exercises are other important properties
 of [lseg]. *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard: (lseg2listrep) *)

forall (s : list val) (x : val),
lseg s x nullval |-- listrep s x


forall (s : list val) (x : val),
lseg s x nullval |-- listrep s x

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 1 star, standard: (listrep2lseg) *)

forall (s : list val) (x : val),
listrep s x |-- lseg s x nullval


forall (s : list val) (x : val),
listrep s x |-- lseg s x nullval

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)


forall (s : list val) (x : val),
lseg s x nullval = listrep s x


forall (s : list val) (x : val),
lseg s x nullval = listrep s x

  
s: list val
x: val
lseg s x nullval = listrep s x

  
s: list val
x: val
lseg s x nullval |-- listrep s x
s: list val
x: val
listrep s x |-- lseg s x nullval

  
s: list val
x: val
lseg s x nullval |-- listrep s x
 apply lseg2listrep.
  
s: list val
x: val
listrep s x |-- lseg s x nullval
 apply listrep2lseg.
Qed.

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard: (lseg_lseg_inv) *)

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x z : val),
lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z
|-- EX y : val, lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x z : val),
lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z
|-- EX y : val, lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard: (loopy_lseg_no_connection) *)

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y z : val),
s1 <> [] ->
s2 <> [] -> x = y -> lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z |-- FF


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y z : val),
s1 <> [] ->
s2 <> [] -> x = y -> lseg s1 x y * lseg s2 y z |-- FF

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(* ================================================================= *)
(** ** Additional exercises: loop-free list segments *)

(** In the following exercise, try to redo the proof above
  using a different partial-linked-list predicate.

 We have mentioned that the [lseg] predicate allows a loopy structure,
 which is quite counterintuitive. Here is an alternate definition that
 prohibits loops: *)

Fixpoint nt_lseg (contents: list val) (x z: val) : mpred :=
  match contents with
  | nil => !! (x = z) && emp
  | h::hs => EX y:val, !! (x <> z) 
                   && data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x * nt_lseg hs y z
  end.

Arguments nt_lseg contents x z : simpl never.

(** Here, "nt" means no-touch. *)

(** The difference between [nt_lseg] and [lseg] is the extra proposition
  [x <> z] in the nonempty situation. This extra clause in [nt_lseg] 
  prevents loop structures.

  The proof theories of [nt_lseg] and [lseg] are a bit different as
  well. The following diagram shows that the counterpart of [lseg_lseg]
  is not valid! 

        +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+   
  x ==> | a | u ==> | b | y ==> | c | v ==> | d | u ===+
        +---+---+   +- -+---+   +---+---+   +---+---+  |
                      ^                                |
                      |                                |
                      +================================+

  In this example, both [ [a; b] ] and [ [c; d] ] are stored in loop-free 
  partial linked lists but it is not true for their concatenation. In 
  general, if [(nt_lseg s1 x y)] and [(nt_lseg s2 y z)] describe two 
  loop-free partial linked lists, the assertion

    (nt_lseg s1 x y * nt_lseg s2 y z)

  cannot ensure that the structure is loop free. Specifically, the address
  [z] may be used in [(nt_lseg s1 x y)]. In other words,

   nt_lseg s1 x y * nt_lseg s2 y z |-/- nt_lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z 
*)

(** For [nt_lseg], the following proof rules are useful. *)

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard, optional (nt_lseg) *) 

forall (contents : list val) (a x y : val),
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x * listrep contents y
|-- nt_lseg [a] x y * listrep contents y


forall (contents : list val) (a x y : val),
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x * listrep contents y
|-- nt_lseg [a] x y * listrep contents y

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard, optional (singleton_nt_lseg') *) 

forall a b x y z : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, z) y
|-- nt_lseg [a] x y * data_at Tsh t_list (b, z) y


forall a b x y z : val,
data_at Tsh t_list (a, y) x
  * data_at Tsh t_list (b, z) y
|-- nt_lseg [a] x y * data_at Tsh t_list (b, z) y

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard, optional (nt_lseg_nt_lseg) *) 

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (a x y z u : val),
nt_lseg s1 x y * nt_lseg s2 y z
  * data_at Tsh t_list (a, u) z
|-- nt_lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z
      * data_at Tsh t_list (a, u) z


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (a x y z u : val),
nt_lseg s1 x y * nt_lseg s2 y z
  * data_at Tsh t_list (a, u) z
|-- nt_lseg (s1 ++ s2) x z
      * data_at Tsh t_list (a, u) z

(** Hint:  This lemma illustrates the most classic case where aggressive
 [cancel] can turn a provable goal into an unprovable goal.  For that reason,
  you may need to use [entailer] rather than [entailer!] at one point. *)
(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** **** Exercise: 2 stars, standard, optional (nt_lseg_list) *) 

forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y : val),
nt_lseg s1 x y * listrep s2 y |-- listrep (s1 ++ s2) x


forall (s1 s2 : list val) (x y : val),
nt_lseg s1 x y * listrep s2 y |-- listrep (s1 ++ s2) x

(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)

(** Now, we will use [nt_lseg] instead of [lseg] in the loop invariant to
prove [body_append]. *)

(** **** Exercise: 3 stars, standard, optional (body_append_alter1) *) 

semax_body Vprog Gprog f_append append_spec


semax_body Vprog Gprog f_append append_spec


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (if (_x == (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
     return _y;
   } else {
     (_t = _x;
      _u = (_t -> _tail);
      while (_u != (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
        (_t = _u;
         _u = (_t -> _tail);)
      }
      (_t -> _tail) = _y;
      return _x;)
   }) POSTCONDITION


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y)) 
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION


Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y)) 
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x = nullval
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (!! (s1 = []) && emp; listrep s2 y))
  (return _y;) POSTCONDITION

 (* FILL IN HERE *) admit.

Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (listrep s1 x; listrep s2 y))
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (EX (h : val) (hs : list val) 
        (y : val),
        !! (s1 = h :: hs) &&
        data_at Tsh t_list (h, y) x
          * listrep hs y; 
   listrep s2 y)) (_t = _x;
                   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y)) 
  (_t = _x;
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _t x; temp _x x; 
   temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y))
  (_u = (_t -> _tail);
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 
Espec: OracleKind
x, y: val
s1, s2: list val
Delta_specs: Maps.PTree.t funspec
Delta:= abbreviate: tycontext
POSTCONDITION:= abbreviate: ret_assert
H: x <> nullval
h: val
r: list val
u: val
H0: s1 = h :: r
MORE_COMMANDS:= abbreviate: statement
LOOP_BODY:= abbreviate: statement
semax Delta
  (PROP ( )
   LOCAL (temp _u u; temp _t x; 
   temp _x x; temp _y y)
   SEP (data_at Tsh t_list (h, u) x; 
   listrep r u; listrep s2 y))
  (while (_u != (tptr tvoid) (0)) {
     LOOP_BODY
   }
   MORE_COMMANDS) POSTCONDITION

 (** Now use [forward_while] to verify this while loop.  Remember,
  [forward_while] will generate four proof goals: current precondition 
  implies loop invariant; loop test is safe to execute; loop body preserves
  invariant; and the correctness of after-loop commands. *)
(* FILL IN HERE *) Admitted.
(** [] *)
